examples.title: 0/1 Knapsack - Bottom Up Tabulation
examples.description: Standard DP approach filling table iteratively
examples.code:
// n items, W capacity, arrays wt[0..n-1], val[0..n-1]
KNAPSACK(wt, val, n, W):
 // Create 2D table K[n+1][W+1]
 K = array of size (n+1) × (W+1)
 
 // Initialize base cases
 FOR i = 0 TO n:
 K[i][0] = 0
 FOR w = 0 TO W:
 K[0][w] = 0
 
 // Fill table bottom-up
 FOR i = 1 TO n:
 FOR w = 1 TO W:
 IF wt[i-1] ≤ w:
 K[i][w] = MAX(K[i-1][w],
 K[i-1][w-wt[i-1]] + val[i-1])
 ELSE:
 K[i][w] = K[i-1][w]
 
 RETURN K[n][W]
examples.explanation: Builds solution from smaller subproblems. Time O(n×W), Space O(n×W). Base cases represent no items or zero capacity.

examples.title: 0/1 Knapsack - Space Optimized 1D Array
examples.description: Reduced space complexity using single row
examples.code:
// Space optimized version
KNAPSACK_OPTIMIZED(wt, val, n, W):
 dp = array of size (W+1) initialized to 0
 
 FOR i = 1 TO n:
 // Traverse right to left to avoid reuse
 FOR w = W DOWNTO wt[i-1]:
 dp[w] = MAX(dp[w],
 dp[w-wt[i-1]] + val[i-1])
 
 RETURN dp[W]
examples.explanation: Key insight: process capacity from right to left so each item contributes at most once. Space reduced to O(W).

examples.title: 0/1 Knapsack - Memoization (Top-Down)
examples.code:
// Initialize memo table with -1
memo = 2D array of size (n+1) × (W+1) filled with -1

MEMOIZED_KNAPSACK(i, w):
 // Base case
 IF i == 0 OR w == 0:
 RETURN 0
 
 // Check if already computed
 IF memo[i][w] != -1:
 RETURN memo[i][w]
 
 // Compute and store
 IF wt[i-1] ≤ w:
 memo[i][w] = MAX(
 MEMOIZED_KNAPSACK(i-1, w),
 MEMOIZED_KNAPSACK(i-1, w-wt[i-1]) + val[i-1]
 )
 ELSE:
 memo[i][w] = MEMOIZED_KNAPSACK(i-1, w)
 
 RETURN memo[i][w]
examples.explanation: Only computes required subproblems. Solves only needed states, may be faster when not all states needed.

examples.title: Fractional Knapsack - Greedy Approach
examples.description: Optimal greedy algorithm for fractional knapsack
examples.code:
// Fractional Knapsack using Greedy
FRACTIONAL_KNAPSACK(wt, val, n, W):
 // Create array of ratios
 items = []
 FOR i = 0 TO n-1:
 ratio = val[i] / wt[i]
 items.append({index: i, weight: wt[i], value: val[i], ratio: ratio})
 
 // Sort by ratio descending
 SORT items BY ratio DESC
 
 totalValue = 0
 remainingCapacity = W
 
 FOR each item in items:
 IF item.weight ≤ remainingCapacity:
 totalValue += item.value
 remainingCapacity -= item.weight
 ELSE:
 // Take fraction
 fraction = remainingCapacity / item.weight
 totalValue += item.value * fraction
 BREAK
 
 RETURN totalValue
examples.explanation: Greedy works because of greedy choice property. Sort by value/weight ratio, pick highest first. Time O(n log n).
